plan.description: Convert all 15 Prisma interactive transactions to batch transactions for PgBouncer compatibility on Vercel + Supabase.

task.name: Convert income POST to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-transactions/route.ts`, refactor the POST handler (line 188). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Replace `db.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})` with `db.$transaction([...])`. - The transaction creates an income transaction and increments account balance. - Since the income transaction create includes `include: { account, incomeType, tags }` for the response, and batch results are indexed by position: put the create as the last operation, extract it from results via `results[results.length - 1]`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's POST handler.

task.name: Convert income DELETE to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the DELETE handler (line 271). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client` if not already imported. - Replace the interactive transaction with a batch: `db.$transaction([db.incomeTransaction.delete(...), db.financialAccount.update(...)])`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`. - The delete result is not used (response is a success message), so no need to extract from results.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert expense DELETE to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the DELETE handler (line 357). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client` if not already imported. - Build the operations array conditionally BEFORE calling `$transaction`: - If `!existingExpense.isSystemGenerated`, push the balance increment operation. - Always push the delete operation. - Replace with `db.$transaction(operations)`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert transfer DELETE to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the DELETE handler (line 373). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client` if not already imported. - Build the operations array conditionally BEFORE calling `$transaction`: - Always push: fromAccount increment (reverse), toAccount decrement (reverse). - If `existingTransfer.feeAmount` and `existingTransfer.feeExpenseId`: push fromAccount fee increment and feeExpense delete. - Always push: transfer delete. - Replace with `db.$transaction(operations)`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert cron process-installments to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/cron/process-installments/route.ts`, refactor the transaction inside the for loop (line 64). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Replace `db.$transaction(async (tx) => {...})` with a batch of 3 operations: 1. `db.financialAccount.update(...)` -- decrement monthly amount 2. `db.expenseTransaction.update(...)` -- update master installment record 3. `db.expenseTransaction.create(...)` -- create payment record - All `tx.` references become `db.`. - The `paidCount` calculation stays outside (it uses data from the `installment` variable, not from within the transaction).
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file.

task.name: Convert income PATCH to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the PATCH handler (line 117). - Move ALL the `updateData` building and conditional balance logic OUTSIDE the `$transaction` callback. - Build a `PrismaPromise[]` operations array based on the resolved conditions: - If amount changed + account changed: push 2 balance updates (reverse old, apply new). - If amount changed + same account: push 1 balance update (apply diff). - If account changed + amount unchanged: push 2 balance updates (move amount). - Push the `incomeTransaction.update` as the last operation (with `include` for the response). - Call `db.$transaction(operations)` and extract the last result as `updatedTransaction`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's PATCH handler.

task.name: Convert expense PATCH to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the PATCH handler (line 161). - Same approach as income PATCH but with installment-aware amount logic. - Move ALL the `updateData` building and conditional balance logic OUTSIDE the `$transaction` callback. - Build a `PrismaPromise[]` operations array based on resolved conditions. - The installment amount calculations (`monthlyAmount`, `balanceDifference`) use only input data and `existingExpense` -- they do not depend on transaction results, so they can stay outside. - Push the `expenseTransaction.update` (with `include: { tags: true }`) as the last operation. - Call `db.$transaction(operations)` and extract the last result. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's PATCH handler.

task.name: Convert expense POST to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/route.ts`, refactor the POST handler (line 256). - Import `randomUUID` from `crypto` and `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Pre-generate the expense transaction ID: `const expenseTransactionId = randomUUID()`. - Move all amount calculations outside. - Build the operations array: 1. `db.expenseTransaction.create({ data: { id: expenseTransactionId, ... } })` -- main record. 2. Conditionally (if not system-generated): - If NOT installment: push `db.financialAccount.update(...)` to decrement. - If installment AND startDate <= today: push balance decrement, push `db.expenseTransaction.update({ where: { id: expenseTransactionId }, ... })` to update remaining installments, push `db.expenseTransaction.create(...)` for the payment record (using `parentInstallmentId: expenseTransactionId`). - Call `db.$transaction(operations)`. - Extract the first result (the created expense transaction) for the response. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's POST handler.

task.name: Convert transfer POST to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/route.ts`, refactor the POST handler (line 209). - Import `randomUUID` from `crypto` and `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Move the fee type lookup (`findFirst` for "Transfer fee") and account name lookup BEFORE the transaction. - If a fee is needed and no "Transfer fee" type exists, create it BEFORE the transaction (it is idempotent/system-level). - Pre-generate IDs if needed: `const feeExpenseId = randomUUID()` (only if fee is present). - Build the operations array: 1. If fee: push `db.expenseTransaction.create({ data: { id: feeExpenseId, ... } })` and `db.financialAccount.update(...)` for fee deduction. 2. Push `db.transferTransaction.create(...)` with `feeExpenseId` (or null). 3. Push `db.financialAccount.update(...)` for from-account decrement. 4. Push `db.financialAccount.update(...)` for to-account increment. - Call `db.$transaction(operations)`. - Remove `{ timeout: 10000 }` option (not applicable to batch). - Extract the transfer create result (track its index in the array) for the response. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's POST handler.

task.name: Convert expense types POST to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-types/route.ts`, refactor the POST handler (line 93). - Import `randomUUID` from `crypto` and `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Pre-generate the expense type ID: `const expenseTypeId = randomUUID()`. - Build the operations array: 1. Push `db.expenseType.create({ data: { id: expenseTypeId, ... } })`. 2. If subcategories exist: push `db.expenseSubcategory.createMany({ data: subcategories.map(sub => ({ userId, expenseTypeId, name: sub.name })) })`. - Call `db.$transaction(operations)`. - After the transaction, re-read the expense type with subcategories: `const result = await db.expenseType.findUnique({ where: { id: expenseTypeId }, include: { subcategories: { orderBy: { name: 'asc' } } } })`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's POST handler.

task.name: Convert expense types PATCH to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/expense-types/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the PATCH handler (line 90). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Build the operations array dynamically: 1. Push `db.expenseType.update(...)` for the type itself. 2. If `subcategoryChanges.toCreate` has items: push `db.expenseSubcategory.createMany(...)`. 3. If `subcategoryChanges.toUpdate` has items: push one `db.expenseSubcategory.update(...)` per item. 4. If `subcategoryChanges.toDelete` has items: push `db.expenseSubcategory.deleteMany(...)`. - Call `db.$transaction(operations)`. - After the transaction, re-read: `const result = await db.expenseType.findUnique({ where: { id }, include: { subcategories: { orderBy: { name: 'asc' } } } })`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's PATCH handler.

task.name: Convert expense types DELETE to batch transaction
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert transfer types DELETE to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/transfer-types/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the DELETE handler (line 150). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Same pattern as expense types DELETE: - Move find-or-create "Uncategorized" transfer type BEFORE the transaction. - Remove the unnecessary `count` check. - Build batch: `db.$transaction([db.transferTransaction.updateMany(...), db.transferType.delete(...)])`. - Extract `updateMany` result from batch results for `reassignedCount`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert income types DELETE to batch transaction
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-types/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the DELETE handler (line 150). - Import `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Same pattern as expense types DELETE and transfer types DELETE: - Move find-or-create "Uncategorized" income type BEFORE the transaction. - Remove the unnecessary `count` check. - Build batch: `db.$transaction([db.incomeTransaction.updateMany(...), db.incomeType.delete(...)])`. - Extract `updateMany` result from batch results for `reassignedCount`. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's DELETE handler.

task.name: Convert transfer PATCH to batch transaction
task.action:
In `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, refactor the PATCH handler (line 138). This is the most complex transaction with fee management and account balance adjustments.
- Import `randomUUID` from `crypto` and `PrismaPromise` from `@prisma/client`. - Move ALL lookups that were inside the transaction to BEFORE it: - The `findFirst` for "Transfer fee" expense type. - The `findUnique` for account name (used in fee expense description). - If no "Transfer fee" type exists and one is needed, create it before the transaction. - Resolve ALL conditions before building the operations array: - Determine `effectiveFromAccountId`, `effectiveToAccountId`, amounts, fee changes. - Determine which fee scenario applies: fee added, fee removed, fee updated, or no fee change. - Determine whether accounts changed or just amount changed. - Build the `PrismaPromise[]` operations array based on resolved conditions: - Fee operations (create/delete/update fee expense + related balance adjustments). - Account balance operations (reverse old + apply new, or apply diff). - The transfer update itself (last operation, with `include` for the response). - For "fee added" scenario: pre-generate fee expense ID with `randomUUID()` so the transfer update can reference `feeExpenseId`. - Call `db.$transaction(operations)`. - Remove `{ timeout: 10000 }` option (not applicable to batch). - Extract the last result (the transfer update) for the response. - All `tx.` references become `db.`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Confirm no `async (tx)` pattern remains in this file's PATCH handler. Grep the entire `src/app/api/` directory for `async (tx)` to confirm zero interactive transactions remain in the codebase.

task.name: Codebase-wide verification
task.action: - Run a grep across `src/app/api/` for `$transaction(async` to confirm zero interactive transactions remain. - Run `npm run lint` for the full project. - Run `npm run build` for the full project. - Generate `docs/batch-transactions-verification.md` documenting all conversions and test results.
task.verify: Grep returns zero results. Lint passes. Build passes.
